title: AgingCard
description: Consolidated per-run output of an AgingBench scenario. Schema version 1.0.0.

schema_version.description: SemVer. v1 ships 1.0.0. Bumping major requires a migration utility.

cost_and_efficiency.description: Operational metadata. Tokens and calls are CANONICAL (every AgingCard should populate them when an LLM was called); latency and cost_usd are ADVISORY (recorded only when callers instrument per-call timing/cost). Latency varies with network and provider load and is not an aging signal; cost_usd is a function of tokens times provider-specific pricing that labs typically compute against their own negotiated rates. Tokens + calls are the universal cost signal across providers; absence of latency/cost_usd is expected and not a defect.

total_input_tokens.description: Canonical. Sum of input tokens across all LLM calls.

total_output_tokens.description: Canonical. Sum of output tokens across all LLM calls.

total_response_tokens.description: Optional. Some scenarios track response tokens separately.

tokens_per_session_mean.description: Canonical. (input + output) / n_sessions; the per-session token budget.

total_calls.description: Canonical. Number of LLM calls made across the run.

total_cost_usd.description: Advisory. Sum of per-call cost in USD if callers record it; typically null. Labs computing their own cost should multiply tokens by their negotiated provider rate.

latency_ms_p50.description: Advisory. P50 LLM-call latency in milliseconds if callers record per-call duration; typically null. Latency is dominated by network and provider load (Tier 1) or tool execution outside the LLM call (Tier 2), and is not an aging signal.

latency_ms_p95.description: Advisory. P95 LLM-call latency in milliseconds.
